title: JSON Decoding, DTOs, Domain Types, Unknown Fields, and Schema Drift · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A regional field-research team incrementally builds typed PokéAPI DTO-to-domain admission boundary from original fixed PokéAPI fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP, Git, quality-gate, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of typed PokéAPI DTO-to-domain admission boundary while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugg…
objectives: Define narrow transport structs with exact JSON tags, nested shapes, optionality, and only fields needed by the command.
Choose unknown-field tolerance deliberately, bound the body before decoding, reject trailing JSON, and preserve decode causes.
Validate ID, name, dimensions, stats, types, abilities, URLs, and collection bounds before constructing domain values.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pokedex Go Response Dto
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 9489 during a clean cross-platform package smoke. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile bounded JSON fixtures with typed domain admission and trailing-document rejection; no user-visible meaning may rely on color or cursor motion; then inject one redirect, control sequence, file, timeout, or persistence failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define narrow transport structs with exact JSON tags, nested shapes, optionality, and only fields needed by the command.
